scenario: Lattice Insurance processes 8000 claims per day on Camunda 8.8 SaaS. The insurer wants a branded customer-facing claim intake screen that matches the rest of its portal instead of the stock Tasklist interface, while keeping BPMN user tasks as the underlying work assignment model and Tasklist still available internally for 200 adjusters.
question: Which path lets the insurer build a custom UI while keeping BPMN user tasks as the work source?

options.text: Embed the Tasklist UI inside an iframe and override the CSS with custom branding for the insurer customer-facing claim app
Build a fully custom application against the Orchestration Cluster API and consume BPMN user tasks from it as the work source
Replace the BPMN user task element with a Camunda 7 human task and connect the custom UI to the legacy REST engine
Generate a SwaggerUI client off the Tasklist GraphQL schema and treat it as the production customer-facing UI for claims

a.text: Incorrect. The docs do not present iframe-embedding the stock Tasklist UI as the documented extension path; they point users to a fully custom application built against the Orchestration Cluster API.

b.text: Correct. Per docs, users are not limited to the Tasklist UI and can build a fully custom application using the Orchestration Cluster API while BPMN user tasks continue to drive the work assignment.

c.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 user tasks are BPMN elements served by Zeebe; Camunda 7 human tasks belong to a separate legacy engine and are outside the Camunda 8 Tasklist product.

d.text: Incorrect. The Tasklist documentation directs custom UIs to the Orchestration Cluster API rather than to a GraphQL schema treated as the production customer-facing endpoint.

explanation: Tasklist ships a ready-to-use UI but the docs explicitly state users are not limited to it; teams can extend their case or build a fully custom application against the Orchestration Cluster API while BPMN user tasks continue to drive the assignment of work.
